metadata.description: Process cartridges for Carlin-type refractory gold ore roasting. Pyrite oxidation is EXOTHERMIC — the roaster generates its own heat. Primary risk is thermal runaway causing sintering and porosity loss, which kills cyanide gold recovery.
metadata.author: Kubaba Labs Inc.
metadata.source: Published Arrhenius parameters (Jorgensen 1981, Hu et al. 2006), Nevada Gold Mines operational ranges

kiln_design._comment: Circulating Fluidized Bed (CFB) roaster design — typical Carlin-type gold operation. Conceptually maps to Lahav's stage model: Stages 1-3 = preheating/drying, Stage 4 = roasting zone, Stage 5 = cooling.

calciner._comment: In gold roasting, this is the main roasting chamber (CFB reactor)

burners._comment: Startup burners only — once pyrite ignites, the reaction sustains itself (exothermic)

aperture._comment: Larger aperture than lime — lower gas velocity needed for fluidization

feed_composition._comment: Typical Carlin-type refractory ore: gold locked in arsenian pyrite, high silica host rock

feed._comment: Finer grind than lime — need liberation of sulfide inclusions

air_flows.primary_air_comment: Fluidization air — blown through distributor plate at bottom of CFB
air_flows.secondary_air_comment: Staged combustion air injected above bed to control temperature
air_flows.tertiary_air_comment: Quench air for temperature control — critical for preventing sintering
air_flows.total_system_comment: Lower total flow than lime — CFB operates at lower velocities

particle._comment: Denser than lime particles — sulfide ore vs porous quicklime

thermal._comment: EXOTHERMIC: 4FeS2 + 11O2 -> 2Fe2O3 + 8SO2. dH = -3400 kJ/kg sulfide. Startup burners only needed to reach ignition temperature (~450C). Once pyrite ignites, reaction is self-sustaining.

rotary_kiln._comment: Not applicable — CFB roaster, not rotary kiln

flue_gas_exit._comment: SO2 and As2O3 must be scrubbed. Nevada emission limits: SO2 < 200 ppm after scrubbing, As < 0.5 mg/Nm3.

self._comment: Self-sustaining: exothermic reaction provides all heat after ignition

equations._comment: Gold roasting equations. Key difference: Arrhenius kinetics for pyrite oxidation and exothermic heat generation. Sintering threshold drops from 1100C (lime) to 700C (gold porosity critical).

baker_equilibrium.description: Not applicable to gold roasting — no CaCO3 equilibrium. Replaced by pyrite ignition threshold.
baker_equilibrium._comment: Placeholder — gold uses Arrhenius kinetics instead

terminal_velocity.description: Terminal settling velocity for ore particles in flue gas
terminal_velocity.formula: v_t = sqrt(4 * g * d * rho_p / (3 * Cd * rho_gas))
terminal_velocity.source: Standard drag equation for angular ore particles

reaction_kinetics.description: Pyrite oxidation kinetics — Arrhenius model. Rate accelerates exponentially above ignition temperature (~450C). Self-sustaining above 500C.
reaction_kinetics.source: Jorgensen (1981), Hu et al. (2006) — pyrite oxidation Arrhenius parameters

porosity_model.description: Ore porosity loss from sintering. Above 700C, pores close and cyanide cannot reach gold. Time-at-temperature integral determines porosity loss.
porosity_model.source: Thomas (2005) — gold recovery vs calcine porosity correlation

environmental.description: SO2 and arsenic emission constraints for Nevada operations
environmental.source: Nevada DEP Title V permit limits

sintering_sigmoid.description: Sigmoid activation for sintering/porosity loss. MUCH lower threshold than lime (700C vs 1100C). In gold roasting, sintering = death of gold recovery.
sintering_sigmoid.source: Ore sintering begins at 680-720C — pore closure kills cyanide penetration

wall_temperature.description: Roasting zone temperature — driven by exothermic reaction heat, feed rate (more sulfide = more heat), and air staging for temperature control
wall_temperature.tert_air_comment: COOLING: In gold roasting, tertiary air is used to QUENCH temperature to prevent sintering. Negative scale = more air = cooler.
wall_temperature.exothermic_comment: Each 1% FeS2 in the blended feed raises wall temp by 12C via exothermic pyrite oxidation self-heating. At 8% FeS2: +96C above convective base.
wall_temperature.source: CFB roaster temperature model — exothermic reaction drives temperature UP

tertiary_air_effect.description: Quench air effect on roasting temperature — OPPOSITE of lime (cooling, not heating)

stage_temperature.description: Stage temperatures — gold roasting operates at lower temperatures than lime

calcination_gas.description: In gold roasting: SO2 gas production from sulfide oxidation
calcination_gas.source: Mass balance: FeS2 oxidation at 100 tph produces 12000 Nm3/h SO2+CO2

heat_balance.description: Heat balance for exothermic roasting — reaction ADDS heat instead of consuming it
heat_balance._comment: Negative tert_air_recovery_factor: quench air REMOVES heat (intentional)

abrasion.description: Wall abrasion — less relevant in CFB than rotary kiln

chemical_risk_sigmoid.description: In gold roasting: chemical risk = incomplete oxidation (sulfur residue too high)

aero_risk_sigmoid.description: Aerodynamic risk in CFB — particle elutriation

pressure_accretion.description: Pressure drop from agglomeration in CFB

pyrite_oxidation.description: 4FeS2 + 11O2 -> 2Fe2O3 + 8SO2 + HEAT. Exothermic reaction — self-sustaining above 500C. Gold is liberated from the sulfide lattice during oxidation, making it accessible to cyanide leaching.

sintering_porosity_loss.full_name: Sintering and Porosity Loss (Gold Recovery Killer)
sintering_porosity_loss.description: Above 700C, ore particles sinter (pores close). Cyanide solution cannot penetrate sintered calcine, destroying gold recovery. This is the PRIMARY constraint in Carlin gold roasting — unlike lime where sintering threshold is 1100C.
sintering_porosity_loss.recovery_impact: Every 10C above 700C reduces gold recovery by ~3-5%

do_increase_quench_air.action: Increase staged air injection to cool roasting zone below 680C
do_increase_quench_air.expected_effect: Temperature drops 30-50C, porosity preserved, gold recovery > 90%
do_increase_quench_air.feasibility: Primary control lever — adjust fan dampers

do_reduce_feed_rate.action: Reduce sulfide feed rate to decrease exothermic heat generation
do_reduce_feed_rate.expected_effect: Less heat generated, easier temperature control

do_NOT_increase_insulation.action: DO NOT add insulation to roaster (opposite of lime!)
do_NOT_increase_insulation.expected_effect: Would TRAP exothermic heat, INCREASING sintering risk
do_NOT_increase_insulation.feasibility: Counter-intuitive: in gold roasting, heat LOSS through walls is beneficial

arsenic_volatilization.description: Arsenian pyrite releases As2O3 vapor during roasting. Must be captured by scrubbers. Nevada DEP limits: < 0.5 mg/Nm3 at stack.

carbon_preg_robbing.description: Organic carbon in ore adsorbs dissolved gold during cyanide leaching, reducing recovery. If Keter classifies ore as 'high-carbon' / 'double-refractory', the roasting temperature must be high enough to burn off carbon — but not so high as to cause sintering. Narrow operating window: 620-680C.

high_carbon._comment: Double penalty for low temperature — must burn carbon

argillite._comment: Clay minerals lower sintering threshold by ~20C
